content:
Key Findings

  • Ribble Valley Borough Council recorded a total spend of £38.6M across 13,723 transactions over five financial years.
  • The council's spending increased by £6.0M, representing a significant 111.8% rise from 2021 to 2025.
  • The largest annual spending increase occurred between 2024 and 2025, with an additional £3.2M spent, a rise of 39.6%.
  • Ribble Valley Borough Council engaged with 1,282 unique suppliers during the analysed period.

Understanding Ribble Valley's Financial Footprint

Ribble Valley Borough Council's financial activities provide a detailed insight into how public money is allocated and spent within the district. Over five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26, the council processed a substantial 13,723 individual transactions. These transactions collectively amount to a total expenditure of £38.6M. This comprehensive dataset, published under the Local Government Transparency Code, allows residents to scrutinise council spending and understand its financial priorities.

The analysis of this data reveals the breadth of the council's operations, involving payments to 1,282 unique suppliers. This wide network of suppliers highlights the diverse range of services and goods required to maintain and improve local infrastructure, public services, and community programmes. Understanding the scale of these financial interactions is crucial for assessing the council's efficiency and accountability in managing public funds.

Analysing Spending Trends Over Five Years

A detailed examination of Ribble Valley Borough Council's spending trends shows a consistent and significant increase in expenditure over the five-year period. Total spending rose from £5.4M in 2021 to £11.4M in 2025. This represents an overall increase of £6.0M, or a substantial 111.8%, demonstrating a clear upward trajectory in the council's financial commitments. Such a significant rise warrants closer inspection to understand the underlying drivers and the impact on local services.

The year-on-year figures highlight varying rates of increase, with some periods seeing particularly sharp rises. The data indicates that spending increased by +£775K, or +14.4%, from 2021 to 2022. This was followed by a larger jump of +£1.4M, or +23.1%, from 2022 to 2023. While the increase from 2023 to 2024 was more modest at +£580K, or +7.7%, the period from 2024 to 2025 saw the most dramatic rise, with spending increasing by +£3.2M, or +39.6%. This substantial final year surge significantly contributes to the overall 111.8% increase across the five years.

Financial Year Total Spend Annual Change Percentage Change
2021 £5.4M - -
2022 £6.1M +£775K +14.4%
2023 £7.6M +£1.4M +23.1%
2024 £8.1M +£580K +7.7%
2025 £11.4M +£3.2M +39.6%

Implications of Increased Spending

The notable increase in Ribble Valley Borough Council's spending, particularly the +£3.2M rise from 2024 to 2025, suggests significant changes in the council's operational scope or investment priorities. This 39.6% single-year increase could be attributed to major capital projects, new service provisions, or increased costs for existing services. Understanding the specific areas where this additional expenditure has been directed is vital for residents to assess the value and impact of council decisions on their local community.

The consistent growth in spending, culminating in a 111.8% overall increase from 2021 to 2025, underscores the dynamic nature of local government finance. With 13,723 transactions and 1,282 unique suppliers, the council's financial landscape is complex. Transparency in these financial dealings ensures that residents can hold their council accountable for how the total £38.6M is spent, ensuring that public funds are used effectively and in line with community needs and expectations.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Ribble Valley Borough Council about the specific reasons for the significant spending increase from 2024 to 2025.
  • Attend council committee meetings, as they are open to the public, to hear discussions on financial planning and expenditure.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see details of major agreements and procurement activities.
  • Write to your local councillor about the council's financial planning and how the 111.8% spending increase benefits local residents.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Ribble Valley Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Ribble Valley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
